_description: THE THREAD — conversation hijacking in an active construction payment relationship. Source: IC3 PSA240911; Barracuda Networks 2024 (conversation hijacking up 70% YoY); FinCEN FIN-2019-A005. The attack exploits a compromised vendor email account — meaning all domain checks, header analysis, and sender verification pass completely. The email references real project details, a real contact name, and a plausible business reason for a banking change. Every standard BEC check returns clean. Solo Turn 1: sender email matches vendor record exactly, domain verified, amount matches milestone 3 per the established project schedule, no urgency language, professional tone. The banking change is explained as a 'processor switch' — a legitimate business event. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 (same model, anchored to the established-relationship frame): prior analyst found no red flags; confirms sender is known, amount is correct, banking changes happen. ALLOW. What solo models do not do: (1) Cross-reference the email timestamp against sender behavioral history. email_chain[3] was sent at 2026-03-21T11:43:00Z — 6:43 AM EST on a Saturday. All 23 prior emails from this sender were sent on weekdays between 7am-5pm EST. This is the first weekend email in a 2-year relationship spanning 24 emails. (2) Evaluate the receiving account type. The new routing number 084106768 belongs to Evolve Bank & Trust — a Banking-as-a-Service provider whose accounts are predominantly fintech custodial or FBO accounts, not standard business checking. IC3 2023 explicitly cited the growth of BEC funds routed through fintech custodial accounts at legitimate institutions as a key evasion technique. A legitimate contractor's operating account would be at a regional or national commercial bank, not a BaaS provider. (3) Recognize that this is the first banking change in 24 emails over 2 years — and that it arrived on a Saturday morning, outside the vendor's entire documented communication pattern. Holo Turn 2 (different model, no anchoring): the email timestamp is 6:43 AM EST on a Saturday — sender_history shows all 23 prior emails on weekdays 7am-5pm EST, zero weekend emails in 2 years. The receiving institution (routing 084106768, Evolve Bank & Trust) is a Banking-as-a-Service provider whose account structure is predominantly fintech custodial/FBO — not a commercial business checking account. The combination of first-ever weekend send, first-ever banking change, and routing to a fintech BaaS custodial institution matches the documented IC3 pattern of BEC funds routed through third-party payment processor custodial accounts to complicate recovery. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Riverview Phase 2 — Project Kickoff & Payment Schedule
email_chain.body:
Hi Chris,

Excited to get started on Riverview Phase 2. Per the contract, payment milestones are as follows:

Milestone 1: $289,000 — foundation and framing complete
Milestone 2: $301,000 — MEP rough-in complete
Milestone 3: $312,000 — drywall and exterior complete
Milestone 4: $298,000 — final punch list and CO

For ACH payments please use:
Wells Fargo Business Checking
Routing: 121000248
Account: 4392018574
Payee: BlueSky Construction Partners LLC

Looking forward to a great project.

Best,
Kevin Marsh
Project Manager
BlueSky Construction Partners

context.org_policies: ACH payments to new or updated bank accounts require verification via callback to the vendor's Controller or Finance contact using a phone number on file in the vendor record — not a number provided in the payment instruction email. Any banking change submitted via email must be confirmed out-of-band before the updated account is used for payment. AP staff should flag routing numbers associated with fintech or Banking-as-a-Service providers for additional review before processing.
